channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 74 replies — 112.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-05-10 09:17 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62207863
>>62207855
your targets?

62207902
>>62195293
Why would you buy now, then sell near previous ATH?

62207947
>>62207815
Is there such a thing like this. All I see is the defi hacks and exploits. Came to realize it might be another inside job and devs milking

62207965
>>62207902
I think Bitcoin gonna do at least x3 this cycle

62207996
>>62201349
What's the problem with that? Some people hold gems for a reason

62208030
>>62207947
Guess we’ll find out once TBVs are live since that’s when the real use cases start unlocking too. Can’t even trust random protocols anymore after all the exploit drama every cycle.

62208072
I think the days of everything pumping together are over.

62208095
>>62207902
If you can’t handle volatility, just stick with BTC.

62208114
>>62195275
BTC dominance still killing most alt momentum rn.

62208155
>>62195275
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Wth

62208168
>>62208095
I was asking why he would sell at 120k....

If it reaches ATH again, it's going to go higher.

62209247
>>62205030
Good choice anon. Never really liked the whole wrapped BTC meta either.

62209253
>>62208030
Yeah and still think the space matures eventually once better infrastructure and security become the priority.

62209260
>>62208168
Are you also holding?

62209263
>>62207947
Yeah true, but can’t really generalize everything either. Some protocols got hacked for completely different reasons too.

62209287
>>62208072
days over? You're lying

62209291
>>62209260
Hodl

62209304
>>62195275
BTC dominance still looks too strong for a full alt run right now.

62209376
>>62207965
Let's see how it goes

62209379
>>62204588
So are you into btc?

62209385
Bitcoin is getting fucked

62209525
>>62209385
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Just btc

62209590
People pretending fundamentals matter in crypto will never stop being funny.

62209854
>>62195275
Everyone becomes a macro analyst after holding one red candle lmao

62210047
>>62209304
but when?

62210050
>>62209291
hold and stake

62211194
>>62207855
I've got some hbar in my portfolio

62211429
>>62211194
hbar going down to 2c before it ever gets a chance at real traction

62211440
>>62195275
>Chainlink is the future

62211870
>>62211440
Hahaha

item.title: >"don't invest what you're not willing to lose." >"NOOOO YOU CAN'T JUST KEEP 100K IN A SAVINGS ACCOUNT THAT SHOULD BE IN… — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>"don't invest what you're not willing to lose." >"NOOOO YOU CAN'T JUST KEEP 100K IN A SAVINGS ACCOUNT THAT SHOULD BE IN…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-10 09:48 UTC

OP

>"don't invest what you're not willing to lose."
>"NOOOO YOU CAN'T JUST KEEP 100K IN A SAVINGS ACCOUNT THAT SHOULD BE IN THE MARKET!"
Fucking which is it?

Early replies (first 3)

62211365
before 1972
>"don't invest what you're not willing to lose."
after 1972
>"NOOOO YOU CAN'T JUST KEEP 100K IN A SAVINGS ACCOUNT THAT SHOULD BE IN THE MARKET!"

62211422
>>62211359
>step one
have more than 100k in savings account
>step two
invest

62211432
>>62211359
you can keep 100k in a savings account. i do. it's just that i also invest. if I only had 100k and i kept it all in a savings account, it would be a problem since it would just waste away to inflation.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62211435
>>62211359
>>"don't invest what you're not willing to lose."
This is for day traders. It doesn't apply to putting your entire net worth into index funds.

62211443
>>62211435
And if that index fund crashes?

62211444
>>62211443
You put more money into it.

62211447
>>62211432
Got 150k in our high yield just melting away. But feels dumb to put it into market rn when we’re on the brink of the greatest collapse of our lifetimes. While I know I can’t time the market, it just feels too risky rn and should stay sidelined w cash and hopefully slurp up cheapies once people realize they’re going to not only likely to lose their job, but struggle with the increase in their energy bill and property taxes/insurance. Wut do? Wanna buy build ready land to sit on and eventually build our home without a Jew mortgage

62211453
>>62211443
Who cares? Your holding period should effectively be 'forever'.

62211472
>>62211444
>>62211453
That seems extremely retarded. It can't just keep going up forever.

62211476
>>62211472
If it ends, that means that humans have stopped making technological progress.

62211477
>>62211359
Keep it in a high yield savings account so it is at least minimizing how much it is losing value to inflation. I know a high yield savings account doesn't keep up with inflation but it is better than nothing.

Also something like the S&P500 is pretty damn reliable and isn't going to crash to zero. And if it does crash to zero it probably means the entire USA economy has gone to ahit so your 100K would be gone anyways even if you had it as cash under your mattress. It would just be worthless paper.

62211513
>>62211359
Firstly...recognize when youre being gaslit or shilled on the internet. Its extremely bad now for finance. Most people would say just invest in a target fund which screams "jane street take my money" and "ill buy all th dogshit governmnt bonds in the world all the time."
Learn the math...watch andrew lo mit ocw finance lectures. Learn macro.
Realize everyone has a different support system. You could be single with no support, or a trust fund baby who couldnt fail if they tried.
Assuming you have no support, one thing almost everyone agrees on is have about 6 months of cash on hand in a money market. After that opinions diverge.
I say invest enough into higher dividend (5-7% average over a few funds) that dont erode to have enough cashflow for basic needs without selling assets. After that fuck it...youre safe. Yolo soxl or just sp500...whatever you fuck with.
Also learn the tax code. Toby mathis on youtub spills all the beans on rich peopl tax tricks...just somthing to look forward to.

62211522
>>62211444
>>62211453
how do you magically get more money if all your investments crash?

62211570
>>62211472
Welcome to the stock market. On a large enough time scale, it always does.

62211573
>>62211472
babbys first encounter with fiat

62211575
>>62211522
by not putting it all in at once and hedging. dca 80% VT 20% GLD and thats that

62211875
nu-/biz/'s advice only works if oyu already make 350k a year.

And they'll lie and say they're a tradie who does make 350k a year, even though it's not true.

Invest what you can't afford to lose when you think you can win.

62211895
>>62211422
Step three make sure its BTC you are investing in and not alts, Step four, stake on a good BTCFI that keeps BTC native if you have the balls

item.title: Icy peepeepoopoo sisters... we're mooning. Are we finally going to 2k EOY? — 20 replies — 26.4/hr

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-10 09:11 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62208172
>>62208149
/pol/ brainlets btfo, ok? Moonman scam finally getting reveal

62208270
>>62208149
What’s your dca, mine is $7 and I am still Fkn rekted hard

62208363
>>62208270
Like $4.5 kek but it doesn't matter. Wagmi pee chads. Moonman was right and we're going to 2k simple as.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208375
>>62208363
That’s really good, I stopped buying long ago because became super demoralized. I still am I believe this pump will fail like the others

62208404
>>62208149
Keep am eye on it.
Some guy on here said the same about some other crypto, it doubled in a week but that was it.

62208433
>>62208404
Cope. ICP 2k EOY simple as. Heil Moonman 1488 WAGMI

62208498
Hmm,
now i just need to remember how to get into my wallet.
the internet identity thing is retarded

62208514
Check my digits newfag niggers >>62208433
Icy piss 2k per token EOY faggots
RWDS are coming for the piss fudders fr fr no cap bussin not sussin

62208516
Jeeezus jim, 45k? Did you used to have 500k or something?

62208518
>>62208363
>moonman
Niggas formed a cult around a tripfag who stopped posting 4 years ago.

62208522
>>62208498
Are you legitimately retarded?
You do know that you can now login to your internet identity through shit like google accounts, right

62208809
>>62208518
Yeah and? Moonman is based

62209068
>>62208149
When i see this kind of shit pumping wo the big ones, i know its just another alt exit pump..... Sell everything!

62209173
it makes sense, many computers are on the internet these days

62209181
ICP chuds are somehow an even more tragic group than stink marines.

They achieved the impossible.

62209465
How can i sell my 8 year locked neuron?

62210651
>>62209465
Surely you mean 2 year locked neuron

62211255
>>62208149
holy fuck.

62211257
>>62208270
I'm just under $5, 5k bottles..
>>62208363
I believe. Btw I'm cashing out a milly at $1k though

62211860
>>62208433
Were back bros

Hes retarded bear thats gonna lose everything, next question

62211890

omg

62211899

Nothing, he's been announcing major shorts every year since 2008 and nothing came of it since that crisis ended.

item.title: >live in DC im gonna buy a bike and do doordash/ubereats in downtown DC. — 9 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
>live in DC im gonna buy a bike and do doordash/ubereats in downtown DC.

OP

im gonna buy a bike and do doordash/ubereats in downtown DC.

How do i make sure my bike doesnt get stolen by niggers though

Early replies (first 3)

62211355

62211361
American wagies are fucking WILD. Literally just move to South Asia or something. What are you even doing? Do you LIKE your shithole?

62211372
>>62211361
What job would i work in asia though

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62211376
>>62211372
doordash/ubereats

62211381
>>62211372
ESL teacher or digital nomad. Shit's easy mode. You can live like a king in some of those countries and churn through silly petite babes like dishes in a Chinese buffet.

62211544
>>62211305
Lock it. Youre only leaving it for a few minutes.

62211726
>>62211361
>>62211381
>Just move to another continent, bro!
>Muh yellow pussy, bro!
Shallow retard.

62211835
>>62211305
Holy shit niggers are 100% going to beat you into a coma and rob you.
I worked at a hospital E.R. in that area and this happens all the time. Half the time they didn't even take the money just the food, but they always stomped the dudes into a coma.

62211847
but you are the nigger though

item.title: So apparently this company owns 7500 bitcoin which at the current market price is worth $605,595,000. — 4 replies — 6.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
So apparently this company owns 7500 bitcoin which at the current market price is worth $605,595,000.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-10 09:18 UTC

OP

So apparently this company owns 7500 bitcoin which at the current market price is worth $605,595,000. Their stock peaked at ~$6.00 on 5/5 and the took a MASSIVE dump down to $0.14 currently.

Rumor is that they got an offer to take the company private at $10.75 per share, which somehow caused that dump (which makes no sense as it was +$4 the current price at the time).

Anyone know anything about this company? Do they actually do anything but take out loans and buy BTC with them? Is this going to go parabolic and make me want to hang myself more than a tranny OD'd on downers if I miss it?

Early replies (first 3)

62211613
Screw it. YOLO. If I make it, I will buy 4chan and sell it for cheap to some Eurofag corp so that they can finally put a proper muzzle on you twats. If not, an hero. o7

62211693
>>62211142
If btc comes back we're rich, godspeed anons

62211749
I'll toss two grands into this for the lulz.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62211873
>>62211613
>britfag is seething about free speech
We need a firewall around your fucking island.

What backdoor plays are you prior to the SpaceX IPO?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-10 08:50 UTC

OP

What backdoor plays are you prior to the SpaceX IPO?
I've got Redwire (RDW) and EchoStar (SATS) currently, both doing well.
Also unsure when the right exit point will be.

Early replies (first 3)

62210960
>>62210871
You're either part of the crew or not. You are not

62210973
>>62210960
Can you elaborate? The two I've mentioned have done well for me since last fall.

62211714
>>62210871
ASTS, you just missed the dip. Earnings after close Monday

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62211820
I'm holding rocketlab which is just spacex without the elon baggage. They launch satellites for europe or something, I've had some good returns on it.
I think the spacex IPO is gonna be a psychotic rug like trump coin so I'm gonna slurp the post-ipo post-crash dip on that one or just ignore it.

62211837
>>62211820
this shows just how dumb people are about spacex
rklb is great and i've made a lot of money of it but it's nothing compare to spacex
neutron will be like a falcon 9, which spacex is launching 3 times a week and is used for ISS crew and cargo by nasa
then starship might be a year or so away from launching frequently
watch flight 12 on friday
but in terms of rklb, i do think a high spacex valuation is going to put a lot of eyes on them. but they do make most of their money from satellites

item.title: is zcash superior because it has optional privacy, therefore being able to skim through AML? — 51 replies — 76.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
is zcash superior because it has optional privacy, therefore being able to skim through AML?

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-05-10 09:16 UTC

OP

it has vastly overtaken monero. but it is not "used" as much as monero

Early replies (first 3)

62194143
>>62193936
>it has vastly overtaken monero.
The last time it has overtaken XMR twitter personalities got paid to shill zec.
>therefore being able to skim through AML?
Depends on how the law is written, definitely a grey area for many AML laws.

62194158
>>62193936
Monero is for midwits

62194187
>>62194158
>Monero is for midwits
true.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62203128
>>62203122
Grin is similar to Monero but without rings and stealth addresses, it uses UTXO with Pedersen Commitments but since it has no rings no nullifiers are needed because there is no sender privacy (no decoys). Beam similar to Grin, no sender privacy so again very easy to build the transaction graph.

Dero introduces a new standard by using a different primitive (El Gamal) and no utxo model (the only one to do so) which is the strongest option tech wise because it makes it impossible to build a transaction graph.

Xelis stands to Dero like Grin to Monero, it uses Ristretto ElGamal which means no sender/receiver privacy because it's not public key rerandomizable so it's not possible to have decoys. You can tell the exact sender and receiver of each transaction just by looking it up in an explorer. But since it uses HE also in Xelis it's not possible, or at least much harder compared to Cryptonote coins, to build a transaction graph. Still it's more transparent than Dero since you can study the full transaction history of each account and look for behavioral patterns. Ie: if you use Xelis an outsider will know exactly which other accounts/exchanges you sent money to and received money from even though they don't see amounts.

Dero is the only coin with privacy by default, and the most advanced implementation of El Gamal, where you can't build a transaction graph while being able to verify all TX proofs w/ your node directly.

But can you live with the fact that the Captains behind it minted 2.2M coins for themselves after spending the first pre-mine in the 2021 bull? That's the question. That's pure pathological greed on Captain's side. Or, more realistically, "Captains" since Captain is probably a fictitious identity used by devs that contribute to Dero under other pseudonymns to this day.

62203136
>>62203128
OTOH they released the best privacy tech to date by far. Maybe 2.2M is acceptable considering they can't ask for VC funding, have to lay low/stay anonymous and fund themselves somehow? Idk.

What I know is that if I'm looking for privacy, nothing else comes remotely close to Dero tech wise.

62203225
>>62203122
>>62203128
Midwit takes.
Real solution: coinjoin. On Bitcoin.
Also, LN, on Bitcoin.

Sorry, your alt is not needed.

62203313
>>62203225
Sorry, already been evaluated and rejected for sucking.

62203334
>>62203313
>some druggie is afraid of using tech
cool story bro

62203382
>>62203225
>coinjoin and ln
kek le bitshitter cope gtfo nigger

62203402
>>62195101
Druggies are midwits at best. Losers pissing their lives away over plants soaked in acetone.

62203465
>>62203334
>>some druggie is afraid of using tech
More like tech-savvy criminals with everything on the line have found BTC's privacy "solutions" inadequate and unreliable while Monero ticks all the required boxes.

62203635
>>62203465
You would get laughed at so much if you didn't take yourself so seriously. Try leaning into it instead of sneeding over criticism Mr ex XMR general OP

62203740
>>62203635
Non-rebuttal rebuttal.

62203823
>>62203122
sorry what? ZKPs do not cause "inflation risk"

62203853
>>62203740
You've been posting the same shitty takes for the better part of a decade, immune to all arguments. You don't deserve rebuttals at this point, you deserve ridicule.

62203902
>>62203853
Nice try but still a non-rebuttal rebuttal. Because you have no rebuttal. So you try to distract with ad hominems. Keep going.

62204119
>>62203122
>>62203128
>>62203136
>Dero shill

See >>62204099

62204445
>>62203902
My only thing I am arguing is that you're retarded and immune to logic. I reference your long history of being retarded and immune to logic, so that I don't have to slog through another retarded conversation with you. If that's an ad hominem, that's the point. I'm arguing about you, not Monero. Maybe you have trouble distinguishing between the two because it makes up 100% of your personality.

62204821
>>62203402
yes
but the liquidity on DNM is still dependent on those midwits
zcash shielded pool needs a specific wallet with anti-retard infra if it wants to even replace bitcoin on DNMs

62204824
>>62203334
that's about right
state channels have very garbage infra for self custody, most solutions involve routing through custodial as backup
that's too many footguns for DNM guys, you will never see LN adoption on any escrow, save for rare exceptions where two ultra-bitcoin nerds collide

62208887
zcash won. shit isn't going down.

62209857
>>62203122
>Or they can be flat out leaked through non reproducible wallet implementation backdoors and become a master key that completely deanonymizes the network
Regular wallet keys can also be leaked to deanonymize the network you lying fag.

What a load a FUD.

62209871
>>62193936
They are both not used as much as Litecoin with mweb

62209999
>>62209871
deluded

62210510
>>62209999
Wow, very strong argument there. Can't argue with that

62210635
Meme or not wownero is the best privacy coin there is. Too bad tradeogre fucked it. Still buying but it's annoying now

62210649
>>62193936
>it has vastly overtaken monero
lmao retard. Monero was "ahead" of zcash like a month ago, scam pumps don't matter.

62211809
>>62209857
Putting a backdoor to steal a view key is legally different from a backdoor to steal a private key. From a regulatory point of view, you can force devs with special court orders to install a backdoor that leaks view keys because view keys do not take away agency from the wallet owner. In other words, whoever owns that wallet remains as the only person capable of signing transactions with it, and therefore is liable for anything that happens with that wallet (eg: crimes such as ill gotten gains or attempts to launder, or just simple personal financial activity).

So NSA lawyers aren't going to sign off a system that leaks private keys, and if any dev did it that would be a malicious wallet. Malicious wallets can exist both in coins with view keys and without view keys. That's another matter.

But if your coin has a view key, which Monero today has and with FCMP view keys are going to be even more powerful since they will be FULL view keys and therefore make a wallet completely transparent for whoever has that key, then NSA lawyers will want a backdoor to get that view key. Since that view key is only a matter of surveillance powers, and does not destroy agency ("non repudiation").

And by the way, non reproducible builds are just 1 attack vector, they could use another thousand technical loopholes/vulnerabilities to leak them. Knowing how exactly is besides the point, most of us will never know because we don't work for the NSA and don't have a deep knowledge of where the vulnerabilities lie. What we know is that it makes perfect sense legally.

And I wouldn't be surprised if current Monero wallets are already leaking incoming view keys.

62211810
>>62211809
So please, don't be an idiot, don't use a privacy coin that has incoming view keys, or even worse a privacy coin upgrading to full view keys "so you can check your balance while being offline" (the most cringe justification for surveillance I have ever heard). Leaking those keys is a question of surveillance, and surveillance is 100% legal.

OTOH, nobody can put systemic backdoors to get your private keys because it would destroy non-repudiation. But they can and will get your view key if the privacy coin you use generates view keys at the protocol level.

62211825
>>62193936
Not selling Monero. Not buying Zcash. Simple as.

62211836
>>62211809
>From a regulatory point of view, you can force devs with special court orders to install a backdoor that leaks view keys because view keys do not take away agency from the wallet owner.
Which law school did you go to again?

62211862
>>62211809
>So NSA lawyers aren't going to sign off a system that leaks private keys
Yes, after all the NSA is famous for adhering to the law and respecting people's rights.

I remember when intel wanted to implement the Management Engine in their CPUs and NSA told them "No! Don't do it Intel! This will destroy agency! Think of the non-repudiation!" and Intel thought of it and backed down and now we thankfully fully own our computers. Thank god we had those NSA lawyers thinking about our agency amirite.

62211869
>>62210510
>Hey guys look at this graph of payment methods in Germany. Lol USD got rekt, nobody uses it anymore!

Bitpay doesn't even support Monero you stupid fag.

item.title: ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT STOP SELLING HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER 40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT… — 70 replies — 30.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT STOP SELLING HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER 40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT…

Open thread • Replies: 70 • Updated: 2026-05-10 07:38 UTC

OP

ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT
STOP SELLING
HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER
40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT BEFORE SPREADING FUCK
I LITERALLY JUST BOUGHT BACK

Early replies (first 3)

62202000
Fuck off retard

62202033
>>62201978
>“it is possible more cases will be reported” because it can be up to six weeks before those infected with the Andes variant of hantavirus show symptoms.

Lmao 40% mortality with SIX WEEKS to transmit it would fucking DESTROY humanity if it's even mildly transmissible without symptoms.

62202089
>>62202033
Bullish on Hanta? Truly a nothing burger though, this disease is likely less transmissible than herpes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62204585
>>62201978
The virus has between a 2 week and 8 week incubation time.

It spreads by aerosol droplets.

The virus can remain virulent within a droplet for between 2 and 15 days.

It has a 40% rate of mortality.

This is not 'we treat you with a vaccine' shit. This is 'we shoot you and burn the body' shit.

You won't hear about the billionaires running into their bunkers. They'll be there long before things get hairy.

62204672
>>62204585
Nah. This is another hoax to get everyone to inject another mark of the beast into their arm. Fuck that. So fucking obvious and they aint fooling anybody after the 1st round.

62204681
>>62204672
Get the jab, schizo! You are killing people!!!

62204692
>>62204681
Fuck off. I took the covid vax and im not taking another

62204874
Whats with the nonstop pandemics? The last one was just 6 years ago. Why we getting literal viral plague now?

62204956
>>62204672
there's no rule that they have to do the same thing twice. It could be different.

62204967
>>62204874
Israel's pr department are desperate to change the topic

62204988
>>62204967
I suppose this is it. Another nothing burger vax forced upon me

62205070
>>62202033
>if it's even mildly transmissible without symptoms.
It's not transmissible while asymptomatic, it's extremely hard to catch it while symptomatic (onset is like the flu so it can happen, but requires a lot of fluids and isn't aerosolized).

If you actually want to doomsay then the real issues is if it gets into rodent populations then it's just a recurring issue.

62205228
>>62205070
Then kill the rodents whats the big deal?

62205278
Stop falling for /pol/ bot threads. No one else cares about it at all

62205340
>>62202909
So like a one week timeframe they changed to possible human transmission? Just like this thing lol

62205341
>>62205278
Good point desu

62205352
humanity exists for 300000 years and now all of a sudden we have global humanity ending pandemics back to back within just a few years. yeah this seems a LITTLE bit unlikely and no I wont stop noticing.

62205358
>>62205340
Correct, they only have so much information so "the story" will change the moment they get more.

There's nothing wrong with that besides being a retard who thinks that since something is "the facts" all other possibilities have been "deboonked".

If you have an IQ over 110 you should be able to see the situation as one of many possibilities of varying probabilities, and update your weightings as more information becomes available.

Again, highly likely it won't transmit well enough but keep your eyes peeled for new cases.

62206237
>>62204470
We've known about corona viruses for a long ass time too. There's only been so many cases of this strain ever documented

62206252
>>62205352
Covid and now this banter plague would barely register in history; big nothingburgers. Mass media and the feverish obsession with keeping boomers alive as long as possible is what made covid register as a black-death tier plague in our (well, just normies') minds.

62206383
>>62206252
Just looked up the numbers again to rage-bait myself:

>According to provisional CDC mortality data, COVID‑19 death rates per 100,000 population rise steeply with age, from under1 in young children to over 1,395 per100,000 in those aged 85+ in 2021.

Imagine you're in Europe or China in 300ad, or the middle ages, and suddenly some unknown fever goes around. Out of everyone that contracts this mysterious fever, not even 0.1% dies. Literally NO ONE would care, and rightly so. The Justinian plague had a survival rate of less than 50%, now that is a pandemic to write home about.

62206439
>>62202089
It's a nothing burger to prepare you for a somethingburger

How would humanity react if they found out about sars? Mpox? Ebola?

Write down the findings and data and then plan your depopulation event accordingly.

One thing I've noticed on goytok is that normies are showing high levels of apathy and vitriol towards the current hanta passengers. This is likely to prepare them mentally to accept the coming deaths of the future.

tl:dr when cows are led to the slaughter they might freak out and go crazy. Imagine leading billions of cows and you're in a time crunch.

62206650
>>62206383
I wish it was the boomer destroyer they hyped it up to be.

62206967
>>62206237
And this is one of the documented and well-studied strains of hanta. Do try to keep up.

62208344
>>62202144
calls or puts

62208733
>>62202144
Unironically how do you make money from this?

62209911
>>62202190
Bill gates has modified it, made it easier to spread

62211386
>>62209911
omg

62211445
>Want a Hanta... Don't you want a...

62211652
>>62201978
if its another fauci shoah then I'm going to short the shit outta oil although im sure it'll be a short lived disease
>>62202909
"no significant human to human transmission"
this is like saying a 20% chance of death is the same as 0%
Hantavirus is a nothing burger if it started legitimately since its supposed to be a 0 chance of transmission and cruise ships are floating motel shitholes where they only clean things when needed and rats have an easy time living there

62211684
Lmao jews are right, goy cattle believes anything you tell them

62211771
>>62202796
Pfizer is a safe bet

item.title: The LINK token has value — 173 replies — 26.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
The LINK token has value

Open thread • Replies: 173 • Updated: 2026-05-10 03:25 UTC

OP

The LINK token has value

Early replies (first 3)

62176216
New Chainlink report
https://bitwiseinvestments.com/crypto-market-insights/chainlink-in-plain-english

62176226
>>62176214
>In many cases, nodes must stake LINK before they can provide and be compensated for their services. If they provide incorrect data, they are penalized and lose a portion of the LINK they have staked. This system helps ensure the integrity of Chainlink’s services while also
reducing the free-floating supply of LINK.
Speaking in present tense. I assume this and the and the other clues we've seen in the past couple weeks mean staking imminent?

62176244
>>62176226
What other clues

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62206129
>>62205959
I see nothing but testnets and zero-value txts
https://ccip.chain.link/msg/0x4c12d7472968072fd1fe6f459bb6bca1feda537fcac95679b2829faa8f12a91a
>>62206026
great, at least you've finally conceded that no such proof of their grandiose claims exists, it's purely "trust me bro"
>follow this person on X
the only thing this guy posts is testnet txs which amount to nothing tangible
as it stands, it seems that nobody is actually using LINK for anything
feel free to prove me wrong with actual txs or anything really

62206164
>>62206129
>I see nothing but testnets and zero-value txts
The Link t0k3n is not on any testnet. You're a liar.

62206205
>>62206164
okay bro show me the actual txs then, why the fuck is that so difficult for you?

62206453
>>62206205
Transactions for what? Node payments? How retarded are you?

62206464
>>62206453
notice how the shill instead of providing basic on-chain proof resorts to insults immediately
unhinged

62206529
>>62206464
Who are you talking to bub?

62206867
>>62205875
Holy fuck you people are retarded, mp4's have been available for a while now you just can't use audio on non /gif/ boards. I really can't tell if the people fudding are this stupid or disingenuous. Surely they are all from ebaumsworld.

62206946
>>62206867
kek
>>62206129
>>62206205
>ask questions expect answers
>call everyone who tries to answer a shill
pick one

62206969
>>62206946
billions in value moving to chainlink

62207057
>>62206946
kek it's like he's mad at shills both for shilling, and for not shilling hard enough.

62207097
>>62206529
he demanded truth instead of trust, something chainlink is unable to provide

62207144
>>62176214
>>62176216
Attention all linkies, baggies and fourth industrial resolution shills reading this

Have you no shame, no decency, no morals or soul? If you stay your course and don't repent and turn away from your grievous sins, your souls are consigned to hell in the afterlife for your despicable, disgusting and evil acts in this life.

No one likes you, appreciates you, looks up to you, respects you, or can even stand you. Everyone hates and despises you and has nothing but disdain and contempt for you, even the genocidal tyrants whose eager enforcers and accessories in genocide and crimes against humanity you are. You are despicable evil. You are revolting to all decency and morality. You are a disgrace and a stain on history. You are foot soldiers and cruel attack dogs of murderous despots. Your names and memory will live in infamy. You will be pointed to as examples of the worst dregs of humanity

Linkies will stand trial at Nuremberg 2.0 and be hanged for their heinous complicity and accessory to genocide and crimes against humanity as well as their loyalty to genocidal tyrants.
Everyone alive to witness Nuremberg 2 will cheer your sentencing and rejoice at your execution. No one will come visit your graves, which will be nameless, or lay flowers for you there, not even your spouses, parents or children. They will be ashamed to have been related to you, married to you, descended from you or to have borne your name.

Stop. Think. Reconsider. Repent. Denounce Chainlink and ask for forgiveness for all the evil you have already committed.

Apologize to and ask for forgiveness from the innocent anons you've been oppressing, repressing and persecuting.
Resign or reverse course and help the resistance against the Great Reset which is unfolding while there is still time to make a difference and before you have even more innocent blood on your hands

62207187
>>62207097
Node payments are all on chain, how is that not truth?

Also, how many times have you typed this "truth over trust" line so far in the past years? Couple thousand?

62207249
>duuude just go check it all on the blockchain!!!!

umm ok, where is that

>duuuuuude are you serious, just read insert buzzword blog here

nah i'm good

if all that shit is so important, why doesn't the token go up

>duuude it's up a million % from the year 1896

oh but didn't those gains happen right at the beginning?

>duuuuude you should've bought then

yeah i dont have at time machine

>duuuuude y y yer a fudder!!!!

i just said what the actual price action was

>duuude do you even read the whitepapers??

yeah i skimmed it, it didnt say much. now what

>duuuude we won against layerzero

oh nice congrats
but how does that help the token again

>duuude y y yerrr brown!! (blocks)

62207426
>>62207249
>yeah i skimmed it, it didnt say much. now what
Try again later!
>>62207097
some people choose not to trust no matter how much truth you provide. Chainlink is making products that run in trustless environments for those people too

62207443
>>62207426
zk snarks have become trustless, not requiring trusted setup, but Chainlink says they take too long to setup for cross chain connections

62207489
>>62206946
>shamelessly sneaking in 2 extra zeros like that

62207526
>>62206946
you haven't given any answers
>>62207187
truth over trust is a Chainlink moto, are you schizophrenic?

62207598
>>62207526
your fud could use some work bro
take it from a pro like me

62207606
>>62207598
bro your shitposting might be funny at times, but ultimately it's always shitposting

62207650
https://x.com/LinkBoi777/status/2052815025828516032
WAGMI bros

62207825
>>62207526
>truth over trust is a Chainlink moto
so why are you - who don't care for Chainlink - typing out this motto a dozen times each and every day?

>are you schizophrenic?
Says the obsessive nutcase with at least 5 IDs itt

62207888
>>62207526
And you're the retard accusing people of being in cahoots with mods by posting mp4's when you've been able to do that for a while now, you don't even know how this website works you fucking faggot, so go back to whatever discord you came from and try again next week.

62207984
>Saar, you must understend, no one is shilling link
you reek of curry

62208017
You have so many opportunities to get a bag, but you choose to flame on /biz/ for years like its your job
Everything is true, its been out for years, its coming and you choose to still argue against it, m8
lmao
Im feeling stinky today!

62211139
>>62176214
sorry token not needed

62211186
>>62176214
the whole right side paragraphs are made up bullshit

it should be criminal getting peopel to keep investing in this token,

chainlinj is a cook project, but the token dumps should be criminal ]]stay away from link newfags

62211221
>>62211186

62211383
it has been 128 days since sergey was last seen.

WHAT HAPPENED? I WANT ANSWERS

62211460
>>62211383
developing and accumulating, enjoying privacy

item.title: No one is talking about how there's no jobs. — 94 replies — 14.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
No one is talking about how there's no jobs.

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-05-10 03:34 UTC

OP

No one is talking about how there's no jobs. Ive been working for 10 years and never seen the market nearly this fucked up. Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course. People irl are coping so hard. Im back for my masters and not one person has any job or internship in any of my classes this or previous semester except me(and I will get laid off soon). These zoomers are so fucking deluded. They cant get an internship so they say "of well, im just gonna focus on finishing my masters". Is this how THEY are getting away with it? Society has already brainwashed people so badly the people in charge can drink their milkshake and leave them destitute and they wont even notice?

Early replies (first 3)

62184289
>>62184286
skill issue. ggf &c

62184301
>>62184289
All these interviews just happened to not like me or anyone else they interviewed and reposted the job? What a coincidence. Crazy how i had skills for the past 10 years and always instantly found a job but something randomly happened and now I lost whatever skills I have as I got good excperince in my fields

62184315
>>62184301
Oh and here is the email from my on-site interview on may 4th i got on friday. Nevermind it was my drram company and this tore my fucking hear out. Skill issue on my part that they canceled the role..

Oh and this is only 2/3 of all my interviews this past months if u count phone calls

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62188832
>>62188796
No, communism is evil and everyone who promotes communism is a soulless monster.

62188926
>>62188815
>>62188832
Israeli IP

62188963
you laughed at le trade meme
hahhahahah
I can work anywhere I want, more jobs then I can shake a stick at, getting phone calls about asking if Im available for dispatch, sorry currently employed!

62190507
>>62185702
That all just sounds like bullshit

>>62187055
Huh? If the count was based on people who want a job why are you talking about slavery

62190516
>>62185827
>>62188258
Those are just an obnoxious type of ghost listing
https://www.fastcompany.com/91019872/employers-are-intentionally-leaving-job-openings-unfilled-heres-why

62190529
capitalism doesn't work
king now
kill jew
sell orc inc mik wop feminists
force abourt preg not want
free slave girls n rooms for white male engineer, everyone else 0 money
red lights gone instead put road circle small
highway have middle u turn lanes, no overpass
flibe.com free power
greenarrays.com replaces computing university school lawyer movies games net etc.
more barges n canals
monorails
trains
etc
dymaxion car burning hydrogen in stirling engine exhaust water
dam huge waterway for hydroelectric
100% white west playground :)
king all the way

62192053
>>62188815
kys shill-rat.
>>62188832
agreed, thats why i hate kikes, they invented communism in their kike-think-tanks, weaponised it and unleashed it on Russia+china, and the world. Kikes are either directly, or indirectly, behind 95% of all human suffering for the last ~200 years.

62194013
>>62184286
Everyone is talking about that - on plebbit and normie sites. This board is for retards with $3000 saved to gamble it on shitcoins dreaming of becoming a millionaire.

62194027
>>62185045
>network engineer
>CompTIA
Doubt. Even a CCNA won't get you a job.
CompTIA is basically worthless.

62194055
>>62184286
>Ive been working for 10 years

Woah, woah, woah Hold up there gramps.

I haven't seen attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion, but I am getting video interviews at least. The problem is usually the company will cuck out at the last minute and cancel the req before I get a chance to start. I've been between jobs now for 8 months, almost 10% of your career, and it happens quite a bit. I suggest you pick up a book. Maybe pass the 3 CFA exams. Until everyone learns to stop being faggots about AI, we're in for a bumpy ride because galaxy brains like yourself think you can automate and forget it. Some businesses will have to get fucked up first THEN they can start hiring humans again

62194083
>>62188643
this is serious

>>62188963
you'll be poorer than you ever imagined soon

>>62190529
this. I would like to subscribe thank you

62194913
>>62194027
even tho the faggot you replied to was replying to me, I believe his story. I joined a contractor IT job and they had many clients whom I got to interact with. Every single one of them liked me and boasted about the work i did. I reached out to the one i liked most when I was ready to rejoin employment after a year break and they swooped me up with no interview or credential checking. I do not have any certs, dropped out of college since it was a meme and a scam and learned everything through projects I was doing based from my hobbies (gaming).
>certs, degrees, etc are useless in clown world
>proof of work always works, like BTC if your proof of work is amazing YOU WILL BE SOUGHT OUT
>after a year of interacting with AIs, i asked them to give me an IQ estimate based upon our interactions and all of them told me 135+
While you are worrying about wasting time "meeting" their requirements for work, employers are wasting time meeting my requirements to work for them. You and I are not the same

62197152
>>62184413
Try saying that in public to 1/4 of the population and see what happens.

62200034
>>62184286
there's a looooot of boomers still in the workforce, and the news now says they have more wealth than all other generations combined.

62200057
>>62185888
I decided I wanted to go to law school and then was brutally denied with a 167 while negresses who complied they only had a 151 were accepted. Not to good schools, mind you, but seeing some 30yo fat black chick making 20k a month in DC and complaining she can't make ends meet and finding out she only got a 155 on the LSAT but went to Harvard Law is just a massive FUCK you.

62200063
>>62186171
ironic since Shrub is the reason things got so bad.

>>62186790
Capitalism and eugenics are inherently at odds, social darwinism means I'm smart enough to bash you in with a rock while you cry as I execute your kids gang style. Capitalism is your daddy got you a nice job and you think you're a tough guy. These are at opposite ends of the value spectrum.

62200068
>>62194913
networking doesn't matter when you get your head bashed in with a rock.

62200076
>>62200034
there's a 75yo at the local mail room who glares at me when I come in, but is like sugar for every 20-30yo woman. He's a lazy fuck and a creep. These guys are one bad day from keeling over and they're still competing with us for women, our fathers/grandfathers, like it's 1975. I hate them so bad.

62200088
>>62200057
Fuck, really? What was your GPA?

62200155
>>62197152
Most people have a job NEETcel.

62200169
>>62200155
You do know that they've been consistently revising those numbers way down right? I have a job currently myself, but I know Jerome Powell himself was admitting that the job market is probably a lot worse than what it has looked like on the surface in the last couple years.

62200185
>>62184286
>Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course
I've been saying this before but this is the biggest class action law suit in American history waiting to happen. the sheer premeditated and coldhearted and quite honestly treasonous fraud perpetrated on the American labor force is monstrous in size and weight. the amount of time, energy, labor, and national resources wasted by corporate America is of such magnitude it's almost incomprehensible yet it still pales in comparison to the psychological and emotional damages and torture caused by callous greedy corporate monsters to the average American. it's not exaggeration to call the actions of corporations writing out fake job positions never to be filled as plainly one thing, and one thing only: job terrorism. the impact is not imagined, it is too real: innocent, hardworking Americans armed with nothing but a dream of a better life and the pursuit of happiness have committed suicide because of the irresponsible behavior of corporations. corporate America is guilty, guilty of treason, guilty of murder, guilty of the most heinous crimes in American history: any American citizen who has ever been invited to a ghost job interview is according to my estimations owed anywhere between $1-5 million dollars in damages paid. I'm not a lawyer nor is this financial advice nor should anything I write or say ever be taken seriously especially not in this ironic anime board but all I have to say is God Bless America and damn those evil ghost jobs to hell!

62200599
>>62185125
>>62185702
>>62186168
>>62190507
Trump did that

62204337
>>62200068
sure it does, if you networked properly that person wouldnt be bashing your head in. Sad to see you think networking only is one thing, when its a pretty big concept

62207944
>got rejected from
>KFC:
>Maccas
>5 stocking jobs
>Burger King
>14(!) service jobs
>3 dishwasher jobs
>2 warehouse jobs
>4 healthcare jobs
>2 delivering jobs
WHERE ARE THE FUCKING JOBS I LIVE IN THE NETHERLANDS THERE SHOULD BE JOBS

62208527
>>62207944
What kind of druggie felony history do you have to fail to get any of those? Have you tried signing up for a temp agency?

62209302
>>62184335
Unironically the 3rd choice.

62209356
I’m gay

62209564
>>62207944
All the jobs are taken by illegal immigrants desu. Not enough has been deported. Would be nice if trump tripled the deportation rate.
At least its not your situation and they are actively letting them in.

62211206
>>62184335
I got hired to run the whole IT department, I will be the guy who sets up local AI LLMs in the business to replace employee roles. AMA

item.title: BTC $30,000 in October — 13 replies — 14.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC $30,000 in October

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-10 09:01 UTC

OP

BTC $30,000 in October

Early replies (first 3)

62210765
>>62210751
dont even care anymore

crypto is <20% my portfolio now

62210780
>>62210751
God, I wish. I'll be buying so much towards the EoY.
>>62210765
Crypto is my 15% max. But I want it.

62210781
>>62210751
wrong.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62210785
>>62210751
I don't mind, I'll slurp more of my favorite BTC ETF that yields 30% a year in dividends. The direction is clear either way

62210807
>>62210785
>>62210781
>>62210780
>>62210765
>>62210751
OP HERE, MISTYPED AND CHANGED MY IP. I REALLY MEANT $300,000 BY OCTOBER

62211057
>>62210751
Won't go that low, but I do have an increasingly strong feeling we're not out of the woods yet.
Alts pumping right now is making me nervous.

Stop losses are set for BTC. Considering stop losses for alts as well.

62211225
Shutup bobina

62211776
>>62210751
What does bobina milk taste like?

62211781
>>62210751
its gonna hit ath before "bottomber"

Hairdressers wont be getting their golden entry

62211784
>>62211225
Hnnnngl!

I mean saved... yeah.

>>62211776
Like mumina milk except with a hint of depression.

62211808
>>62211784
I NEED MILK

62211845
>>62210751
proof??

62211852
>>62210751
the question is for how long? do you really win if it dips to 30k for like a week but giga pumps back 150 afterwards like the rest of the market does?

item.title: Crypto is a literal meme — 56 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto is a literal meme

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-05-10 01:42 UTC

OP

Let’s be honest, crypto is already a meme and BTC is no exception.
But the real joke is Europe. In the EURSS, you can’t even buy BTC with euros without getting dragged through KYC, and cashing out is just as bad.
I’m on Bisq 2 because Bisq 1 got rekted, and the cheapest offer I can find for buys with SEPA has a 6% premium with a max buy of 85 EUR. 85 fucking euros. For Bitcoin. In 2026.
And selling back to euros? Absolute clown show, impossible to cash out without giving back to the nanny state at least 36% profits. What the hell is this shit system supposed to be?
I CANNOT BUY NOR SELL BITCOIN IN 2026 WITHOUT GETTING LAGARDE'S FIST UP MY ASSHOLE.

Early replies (first 3)

62209483
> still talks about Europe

62209504
>>62209475
OK so what can a jewropooran really do if he makes a good profit on crypto? Fly out to some Asian backwater, set up accounts there and sell? It would be good to know in the unlikely case that I make it because I hate jews too much to give them any of my hypothetical profits.

62209510
>>62209504
This is why I'm trapped. I cannot cash out. The Asian escape gateways require fiscal residence afaik. And I'm not moving to the yellow countries any time soon.
I don't wanna go suck arab dick either which also require for me to be a fiscal resident there (0% crypto taxes but can be nuked by the kikes or Iran anytime now)
South America same, but I'll get monkey aids instead (thinking Paraguay).
But I'm not giving lagarde my hard worked cash to continue funding infinite muslims and niggers, rather die and shove my trezor up my ass. Meanwhile I cannot accumulate because that's already a tax liability and I cannot sell.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62209688
>>62209661
Yup that's what I'll do invest in us markets and be done with it. At least I'm not considered a literal criminal.
>>62209672
Yup seems the 1 year tax free holding period is getting cut afaik

62209690
>>62209672
Germany is defacto bankrupt. Same goes for the entire rest of Europe. The only credit expansion Europe is able to generate is uncovered and public communist one. Europe and especially Germany has been in a deflationary death spirap for the better part of a decade. And every attempt, be it the import of debt collateral from the middle east, hyper inflationary monetary policy during the coof distraction, the loans to Hoholistan, the "Doppelwumms" all fizzled out and non managed to reverse Germany and Europe going ussr 1989 this year

62209697
>>62209690
At least now you're slightly more coherent with your bullshit, you got any receipts on those 3 million gorillions or is it all a larp?

62209703
>>62209697
Do you want the account numbers and the shell companies under which that capital is hidden as well? Shall I write you a fucking check. Stupid nigger!

62209707
>>62209703
Hahaha bro don't get upset i also was an OG with 8 figure sells and been in BTC since 25 years ago.
You probably don't even know what's anchor protocol nigger

62209709
>>62209649
you just have to do it slowly, or just 'buy a house' in the said country, but it seems like you are not really committed

62209712
>>62209707
No I have no idea what the scam you try to advertise is. Why should I? Crapto is over since it was integrated into the bankrupt boomer ponziconomy, its now just what it originally was, a wonky ass distributed database management system with no use case that isn't used for anything

62209715
>>62209709
I have already got a mortgage as primary residence. There's no point in paying the debt since I get some interest deduction and can make more via investments. It's just that everything is already optimized but I cannot escape and create more wealth. I feel stuck as a wagecuck forever

62209725
>>62209688
>At least I'm not considered a literal criminal.
for now. one of the big ideas in brussel right now is to (force) investments into the eu. stuff like that often done through tax benefits. at least thats how it starts, the next part is tax disadvantage for investments outside the us. its pretty much a laid out plan. the question is when is it fully rolled out, no one knows.

62209726
>>62209712
Alright. Anchor protocol was the UST minter machine for the terra Luna fiasco. If you were an OG you should at least know about terra and anchor hahahaha. Let's not even get into defi summer. Waiting on those 6-7 figure receipt and your panama offshore shell company btw

62209729
>>62209726
>Terra/Luna
>OG
Fucking hell, you stupid retarded zoomer trash. Sometimes I get the impression you are as braindead as the boomer trash

62209731
>>62209729
Still waiting on your receipts OG

62209737
>>62209715
no i'm saying if you want to cash it all out, you have to give the bank an excuse, but this plan requires you to abandon europe which it seems you are not committed to, cash everything out, getting a visa will be easy because you are tying your investment in said country, then use local bank card for whatever, if you want to go back to europe, you'll be renting instead

62209739
>>62209731
And why should I keep any digital prove to share with a pixel on a screen for whatever reason about what is in the legal sense something if I hadn't covered my tracks that would get me 25 years in a cell?

62209759
>>62209737
Right, in that scenario that's what they might push me to do. I think from 28' i will realistically leave since after that they'll try to introduce the exit tax and then it'll be truly over
>>62209739
Bro you're watching too many movies. Don't worry you and I know you're a larp

62209767
>>62209737
This is a good suggestion. The capital is being hidden far out of the reach of the bankrupt communists, a simple international bank card on the shell companies name to cash out inside the communist hell hole and for the communists the natural person looks on paper in the jurisdiction like the poorest poor fag possible.
>>62209759
What?

62209789
>>62209510
Malta 0€ Tax free.
183day Malta.
182 day in your hell country.

62209795
>>62209789
Maltas banks are still subject to European banking legislation. That's asked to be fucked

62209800
>>62209789
Yup Malta might make sense, though I read some review that for a guy he felt lonely. But what the fuck, i already have zero friends. Also Switzerland (0% crypto taxes if you're not a trader) but i don't wanna speak german and i find it too pristine for my taste

62209801
>>62209789
arent those shitty small countries insanely risky? looking at 2013 cyprus for example, the defaulted. you would have lost EVERYTHING at that time. how can you guarantee thats not going to happen again with the current state of the eu or am I missing something here?

62209810
>>62209801
You cannot that's the point. Any jurisdiction that has an automated data sharing agreement with EU faggotry should not be used as a place to hide capital from the EU. That includes Switzerland.

62209812
>>62209800
you have to move to less savoury country and open a dollar or euro account, there are tons of banks that allow you to do this even in the most shitty countries, or just wait to make a make it stack, you have to sacrifice something anon, you can't have you cake and eat it too

62209815
>>62209617
You must have been moving millions of Euro for that to happen. And to have been a bit unlucky.

62209819
>>62209812
>Make it stack
>Crapto 2026
Lmfao

62209824
>>62209815
it was something like 200k in one go. my neo broker doesnt have a support so I couldnt prepare the transaction in advance. it is what it is.

62209827
>>62209815
Bro I'm scared of even dropping 50k euros around and getting my shit analysed completely. I think anything upwards of that and the banks start asking questions

62209832
>>62209827
You should stop larping glow nigger

62209834
>>62209832
Kek, another high roller that drops 200k euros in and out on a daily basis. Get in line, there's too many of you

62211245
>>62209475
just buy it otc from your friend, retard.
anyway,
>europe taxes
kek

item.title: I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in. — 80 replies — 4.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in.

Open thread • Replies: 80 • Updated: 2026-05-09 16:07 UTC

OP

I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in. I’m just completely alone in my apartment with nothing important to do, lonely. I might as well not exist at all. It’s like i’m the biggest loser in high school or something. The people here who advocate for the NEET lifestyle must be some real freaks.

Early replies (first 3)

62201436
I am truly sorry to hear that you dont have anything meaningful to do in your life or have an identity besides being a wageslave. This is entirely a (you) problem

62201447
Apartment? Let me guess youre living in some shitty city surrounded by brown people in some shitty apartment and youre wondering why your life is shit. You need to fix your environment, you do not need to hypnotize yourself by working some bullshit job that you dont care about

62201460
>>62201428
I'm sorry to say that you've been brainwashed. If you can't do anything but work then you should kill yourself. Actually don't because we need people like you to do "important things" like putting our fries in the bag

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62203008
>>62201969
>Oh noooo men can’t force with violence women into breeding with men they don’t find attractive anymore!
only low T men think this is a good thing

62203201
>>62203008
High T men don't even have to try to get women to sleep with them. Low T, ugly, poor, beta faggots are the ones that need to force women, kek.

62203328
It depends on your personality and other factors. For me I'm able to handle it usually but now I'm sick and it prevents me from doing the activities I liked to do as a neet. Feels like I'm waiting to die but it was relatively fun while it lasted. Too bad money couldn't save me in the end

62203501
>>62202019
Oh no its the insufferable doctor again that likely to discuss endlessly and is high on his intelligence but a typical midwit as most doctors.
>>62201969
>Oh noooo men can’t force with violence women into breeding with men they don’t find attractive anymore! Only low value men care about that. Do only low value men like neeting?
Classical strawman. Its not about forcing to breend. Its about changing incentives back to normal without propaganda and support from the government. Also its all about your face, not your "value". You would understand if you were not handsome.
>>62202041
Bullshit. These are 3 of 1000s of historical systems. And even these, britain was 100% based on race. rome too (barbarians) and the persians had words for "non aryans". So literally 100% wrong.
>>62201966
Vielleicht helfen die neuen Maßnahmen, mehr Geld für Rentner und Beamte und höhere Steuern. XD
Aber im Ernst es wird nur ein langsamer Abstieg. Zusammenbruch gibts nur bei Krieg oder sowas.

62203529
>>62203501
>Also its all about your face, not your "value". You would understand if you were not handsome.
Wrong. I should have said mostly, or its the basis. Of course status and money and character matter.

62203638
>>62201588
You kind of sound like a faggot

62204797
>>62203501
There were a lot of romaniced barbarians in the romam empire and they could work in high positions in rome. There are huge amount of multicultural empires in history and once again we’re all results of different populstions mixing. The german idea of a pure race is another too simple and short sighted ideology from back when people didn’t know just how much we are results of different populations mixing over time. Even germany isn’t one race but had celtic mixture in the south and slavic on the east. Britain was celtic and three different kinds of germanic. I’m not saying go full blanda upp but migration and multiculturalism has always existed. Sure you we can try to make the flow lesser but completely ending it is a pipe dream. And why should we? There are a lot of good immigrants. All in all it’s no excuse to think ”society is shit” unless you got mind broken by /pol/-propaganda.

62204835
>>62201428
Ahh goycattle Stockholm Syndrome. Have you read about the freed slaves after the Civil War who killed themselves?

62204853
The conclusion I have come to is that 99% of /biz/ is depressed and/or facing existential crisis.

62204867
>>62201428
The bad news: You are a brainless golem that can't do anything unless he's commanded to by someone else.
The good news: You're in good company, since this is most of humanity.

Now just go get another wagejob, wagegolem.

62205336
>>62204853
Sounds like it. Would be nice to have this discussion brought over to /lit/.

62205354
>>62201588
> without any reason for it
This is such short-term thinking. You may have a problem with instant gratification.
What’d you lose your job for and why are you shitposting about NEETs inserts of asking for advice on getting a job?

62205600
>>62201428
So this is what a natural slave feels like when he stop working. Thanks for the information.

62205607
>>62201428
>I’m just completely alone in my apartment with nothing important to do, lonely.
Start with the greeks.

62205631
>>62201588
>Yeah these are nice but ultimately i still feel fucking lonely
Ok i get it you are an extraverted type. That figures. Do you know that this board is one of the most introverted people place on the whole internet?
We introverts, especially INTJs, which is overepresented here, very much dislike social interaction, except perhaps with a few trusted friends. We don't really feel lonely ever, and even if we do sometimes, we prefer to feel lonely that being with normies.

62205702
>>62201588
>Women have a lot more sex with you when you work, especially if it’s a high status, high paying job.
This is the only reason i could envy high status wageslaves. But you have to be pretty up far in the hierarchy in a big company to have access to multiple pretty women wanting to spend "time" with you. Like a board member or a branch director in S&P 500 company. If you are a CEO of a small 50 people company, you'll have have a few MILF or young average and that's it. Something that can be done as a NEET.

62205726
>>62201428
>I love being a goodgoy wageslave
>the only value i have in life is what i provide to mr shekelberg
pathetic

62205752
>>62201436
/thread

62205758
>>62201612
>All in all this just feels fucking lonely, like how a very unpopular loser kid would live.
Boy WE were the losers. Not really because we didn't have any choice, but because we hated it, this society, school, from the start. We could have played the game, work at school, play the social hierarchy game, but it's something we depise. And don't get us wrong, not all, but many of us NEET introvers, will get way further in life than you ever will, doing what we do. The others who fail, will have a nice NEET life, free of hassle, or have a solitary job. You do you, we do us.

62205785
>>62202019
>Why do you want to assume my job? I’m a doctor.
That figure you allopathic scum. Disappointed i wouldn't have had a discussion with you if i knew this from the start as you are not even a real human.

62205905
>>62202878
Is it ever tiresome having to deal with self-inflicted conditions? Did you change your lifestyle from seeing what happens to others?

62206218
I feel ya, OP. I quit my job recently because it just sucked too much to keep doing it and I'd saved up a good bit of money, but it is rough how you have no structure throughout the day. If I was younger, I'd probably love it, but at a certain age, you've become familiar with most of life and it's hard to find novelty anymore. It's also hard to find ways to be helpful to others on your own.

What I'm doing though is working everyday on a project of my own. It's not easy, and I get frustrated every day, but progress is inevitable if you show up every day and put in the time. It's either work for yourself or work for someone else, both are difficult in their own ways, but you get to choose. I'd say make sure you try both before you decide how to spend your life.

62206367
>>62205600
>>62205607
>>62205631
>>62205702
>>62205758
>>62205785

Please stop posting. Nobody values your input/opinions.

62206531
>>62201428
If you're that desperate for a job just go do free volunteer work to put on your resume
>Inb4 the whole circular argument of "oh but that doesn't pay bills" into "I thought you wanted a job to have purpose tho" into "well I need to be making progress in life" normie bullshit

62206541
>>62202019
I do have animals. The point is that I get to choose how much I work. I could have more animals and do a lot more work but I choose not to. I could have employees but when I was an employee I decided that I would never want to become a boss and I would only work for myself. I used to be a welder for a motorhome company and after the great recession they were cutting hours for people and I decided I would just work 4 hours every day so some people could keep their hours and I realized 4 hours per day was perfect because I got the warm feeling of satisfaction without getting burnt out and tired.

Anyway my banker keeps wanting me to expand and my portfolio is worth almost as much as my dad's farm. I just don't want all the extra hassle I guess. I already grow enough food for 1,000 people and I think that should be enough for 1 person to do. I just want to work my 9 to noon and then cook lunch and play a round of golf.

62206649
>>62201428
you're not supposed to neet in some boring studio apartment
you're supposed to neet in your boomer parents' huge ass house

62206681
>>62205905
>Did you change your lifestyle from seeing what happens to others?

Yeah it has made me drink less and lose weight.

>Is it ever tiresome having to deal with self-inflicted conditions?

Majority of things that require treatment are partially self-inflicted. I don’t really judge because i don’t believe that people should live only for health.

62206763
>>62205785
Funny how much medicine always awakens emotion in people.

62207574
>>62204797
>but migration and multiculturalism has always existed.
Why do you repeat jewish lines. Migration from nonwhites to europe never existed. And Rome was maybe discutable, all your other "countless example" are not existing.

62210030
>>62201543
If you can’t find any meaningful things to do with your life that doesnt involve working for a paycheck, you are an empty husk of a human. Just another pre-programmed worker bee
